preview_text:
The model favors Iran to win, assigning the team a 49% chance of victory. Egypt's win probability stands at 22%, leaving the underdog substantially behind in the statistical forecast.

The model's draw probability of 30% suggests a competitive match with a plausible stalemate outcome. The gap between Iran's win chance and Egypt's is wide enough to indicate a clear favorite, yet the high draw likelihood indicates the teams are closer in expected performance than Iran's win percentage alone might imply. A draw remains nearly as probable as an Iran victory in the model's assessment.

The market diverges meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both price Egypt's win probability near 43%, roughly 20 percentage points higher than the model's 22%. Both betting sources converge on Iran around 26–27%, more than 20 points below the model's 49%. The draw probabilities align closely across all three sources at roughly 30–31%. This substantial gap suggests the market is significantly more bullish on Egypt than the statistical forecast.
